title: Concrete Chemicals Receives â‚¬350 Million Funding for Germanyâ€™s Largest Industrial-Scale e-SAF Plant
content: Concrete Chemicals GmbH has secured â‚¬350 million in public funding to realise Germanyâ€™s largest industrial-scale Electro-Sustainable Aviation Fuels plant, with national and regional approvals in place and the European Commission review in its final phase. Under the CEEAG framework, the project in Schwedt supports EU energy sovereignty, ReFuelEU blending mandates, and Germanyâ€™s Sustainable Aviation Strategy. Using biogenic CO2 and renewable hydrogen in a Power-to-Liquid process, it targets more than 37,000 tonnes per year, including 30,000 tonnes of e-SAF and 7,000 tonnes of e-naphtha, cutting at least 100,000 tonnes of fossil CO2 annually. The consortium plans local CO2 supply via LEIPA Georg Leinfelder GmbH. ENERTRAG and Zaffra lead the initiative, aligned with IPCEI goals and designed for potential connection to the H2-Kernnetz. Technical planning starts immediately, with final investment decision targeted by 2027 to support 2030 mandates
author: Analysts Account
